response: The global food system faces mounting pressures, with the population expected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050 and food production needing to rise 60% to keep pace. Yet agriculture both suffers from and drives climate change, contributing about 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, while water depletion, soil degradation affecting 33% of arable land, and the waste of one-third of all food further strain the system. Potential solutions include precision agriculture, vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant GMOs, regenerative practices, and policy reforms on subsidies and carbon pricing. The central challenge is scaling these innovations while keeping food affordable and accessible to the world's poorest.
